titleEn: Canadian firms launch world's earliest in-ovo egg sexing system
titleZh: 加拿大企业推出全球最早孵化期蛋内性别鉴定系统
summaryZh: 加拿大三家企业合作商业化全球最早（孵化第4天）的蛋内性别鉴定系统，采用高光谱成像技术无损检测胚胎性别，计划2026年底或2027年初全球上市。该系统可提前淘汰雄性蛋，释放孵化器容量、降低资源消耗，是家禽孵化管理领域的重大突破。
featuredReason: 该技术将高光谱成像与AI分析结合，实现孵化早期无损性别鉴定，是智慧畜牧中传感器与自动化在禽业场景的典型应用，对提升孵化效率、减少资源浪费具有直接实践价值。
contentFull: In-ovo sexing Canadian firms launch world's earliest in-ovo egg sexing system Hyperspectral technology determines sex from day four of incubation 1 July 2026 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media North America Three companies have partnered to commercialise a Canadian-developed in-ovo egg sexing system capable of determining the sex of chick embryos as early as the fourth day of incubation, earlier than any other technology currently on the market, Canadian Egg Technologies announced.SANOVO Technology Group, Canadian Egg Technologies (CET) and MatrixSpec Solutions will support a global commercial launch late this year or early 2027. The system uses hyperspectral imaging to make accurate, non-destructive sex determinations without damaging the egg, and is designed to scale across hatcheries of all sizes."Egg sexing technology is one of the most significant advances in hatchery management in a generation, and this technology sets the benchmark," said Michael Midskov, group CEO of SANOVO Technology Group. "Bringing this to market through a partnership that combines Canadian innovation with SANOVO Technology Group's global infrastructure is exactly the kind of collaboration that moves the industry forward.""This technology was built from the ground up to be practical — not just scientifically impressive, but deployable for hatcheries everywhere," said Michael Ngadi, CEO of MatrixSpec Solutions.CET is a collaboration between Egg Farmers of Ontario and Egg Farmers of Canada. The partnership said earlier sex determination allows hatcheries to dedicate more incubator capacity to viable female eggs and reduces resource consumption across the hatch cycle. translationZh:
三家公司已合作将加拿大开发的蛋内性别鉴定系统商业化，该系统能够在孵化第4天就确定鸡胚的性别，比目前市场上任何其他技术都更早，加拿大蛋业技术公司宣布。

SANOVO技术集团、加拿大蛋业技术公司（CET）和MatrixSpec解决方案公司将支持该技术于今年年底或2027年初在全球商业上市。该系统利用高光谱成像进行准确、无损的性别鉴定，不会损坏蛋壳，并设计为可适应各种规模的孵化场。

“蛋内性别鉴定技术是一代人中孵化场管理领域最重大的进步之一，这项技术树立了标杆，”SANOVO技术集团首席执行官Michael Midskov表示。“通过将加拿大创新与SANOVO技术集团的全球基础设施相结合的合作伙伴关系将其推向市场，正是推动行业向前发展的那种合作。”

“这项技术从头开始就注重实用性——不仅科学上令人印象深刻，而且可在任何地方的孵化场部署，”MatrixSpec解决方案公司首席执行官Michael Ngadi说。

CET是安大略省蛋农协会与加拿大蛋农协会的合作项目。该合作伙伴表示，更早的性别鉴定使孵化场能够将更多孵化器容量用于可育雌性蛋，并减少整个孵化周期的资源消耗。
contentHtml:
Canadian firms launch world's earliest in-ovo egg sexing system 
Hyperspectral technology determines sex from day four of incubation
